prestazioni: Engine swap to a 2.8L AAA VR6 (12-valve) with a Schrick 272° camshaft kit, ported and polished cylinder heads by VW Motorsport, and a custom 6-3-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold by Supersprint with a 2.5" dual-exit system terminating in a Remus Sport exhaust with electronically controlled butterfly valves for backpressure optimization.

Forced induction via a Rotrex C38-91 supercharger (12 psi max boost) mounted on a custom billet aluminum intake manifold with a Bosch 0280155862 850cc/min fuel injectors, paired with a custom ECU tune by HJS Motorsport utilizing a standalone AEM Infinity 7 for VR6-specific ignition timing and fuel mapping.

Suspension overhaul with KW Variant 3 coilovers (8-position rebound, 12-click compression damping) modified for the Golf Country’s extended wheelbase, reinforced subframe bushings by Powerflex (PFB80-611), and a custom rear sway bar by H&R (25mm diameter) to counteract body roll from the raised chassis.
estetica: Full OEM+ restoration with a period-correct VW Motorsport Group B rally livery (white with red/blue accents), including authentic decals sourced from VW Classic Parts and a custom front splitter by BBS (RS010 style) adapted for the Country’s bumper.

Rear fender flares by Zender (Type 3, bolt-on) widened by 50mm per side to accommodate 17x9 ET15 BBS RS wheels with 225/45R17 Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires, paired with custom center-lock adapters by WRC-style wheel spacers from 5Zigen.

Interior retrim with Recaro SRD seats (replica of the Golf Rallye’s shell) upholstered in black Alcantara with red stitching, a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (380mm, suede), and a custom roll cage by Matter Germany (8-point, bolt-in) with integrated harness bar for Schroth Profi II 6-point harnesses.
